meta.title: Server Actions: Beyond the Basics
meta.excerpt: Production patterns for secure, type-safe server actions

props.body: Server Actions simplified our API layer by eliminating the need for separate API routes. But production use requires more than the basics - security, validation, error handling, and consistent response patterns.

props.text: Server Actions are async functions that run on the server, callable directly from Client Components. They replace traditional API routes for mutations, providing automatic request handling, error boundaries, and progressive enhancement.

idealFor: Any action triggered by user interaction
notIdealFor: Public APIs consumed by third parties

props.text: Every Server Action in our application follows a consistent pipeline that handles security, validation, and error handling in a predictable order.

steps.title: 1. Security Check
steps.description: Origin validation and rate limiting before any processing

steps.title: 2. Input Sanitization
steps.description: Neutralize XSS vectors in all string inputs

steps.title: 3. Schema Validation
steps.description: Validate data structure and business rules with Zod

steps.title: 5. Response
steps.description: Return structured success or error response

props.text: Security checks run first, before we touch any user data. If security fails, we reject the request immediately.
After security checks, we sanitize inputs and validate against our Zod schemas. This catches malformed data that could cause issues downstream.
With validated data, we can safely execute business logic. Our forms send dual emails - one to the customer and one to the business.
Server Actions can fail at multiple points. We use a consistent error handling approach that provides useful feedback without exposing internal details.

features.title: Security Errors
features.description: Generic messages like 'Invalid request' - never reveal security implementation details.

features.title: Validation Errors
features.description: Field-level errors mapped to form fields for inline display.

features.title: Business Logic Errors
features.description: User-friendly messages with retry suggestions when appropriate.

features.title: Unexpected Errors
features.description: Logged to monitoring, generic message to user, never expose stack traces.

props.text: All Server Actions return a consistent response structure that the client can reliably parse and handle.

props.title: Progressive Enhancement
props.body: Server Actions work without JavaScript through native form submissions. For the best UX, use the useFormStatus hook to show loading states and useOptimistic for instant feedback on mutations.

Server Actions are powerful but need a consistent pipeline pattern. Always run security checks first, sanitize inputs, validate with Zod, then execute business logic. Use structured responses that the client can reliably handle. Never expose internal error details to users - log them server-side and return generic messages.
